title: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 30 10
display_title: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 30 10
wikitext:
[]: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Concerns » Fri Aug 20, 2021 8:24 am I'm talking about this: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Archer » Fri Aug 20, 2021 8:35 am This is discussed a bit in, I wanna say the train station thread? And I think the general consensus reached was that Rei Q and Rei 2 shared a soul (since Kaworu mentions that Rei Q can’t be used to grab the spears because her soul is “in the wrong place” - possibly implying it’s still trapped in Unit-01). Thus, after Rei Q dies, Rei 2 inherits some of her feelings and emotions. It’s not a perfectly satisfying explanation to me, since I’d rather 2 and Q just be kept as completely separate entities with no confusion between them, but based on the textual evidence (and some precedent from NGE, where Rei 3 inherits some of Rei 2’s feelings but not her memories due to having Rei 2’s soul in her body) it seems likely that this is what they were trying to imply.

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by RussianRiz » Fri Aug 20, 2021 8:44 am This doll has the name Tsubame written on it. It's a strong indication of connection between Rei (tentative name) and Rei II, either by the quantum mechanics present in Rei, or by the dynamics of the anti-universe. It's the best we can imagine.

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri Aug 20, 2021 8:58 am Archer wrote: It’s not a perfectly satisfying explanation to me, since I’d rather 2 and Q just be kept as completely separate entities with no confusion between them, but based on the textual evidence (and some precedent from NGE, where Rei 3 inherits some of Rei 2’s feelings but not her memories due to having Rei 2’s soul in her body) it seems likely that this is what they were trying to imply.

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:06 am Whatever the reason I tell you this, anyone saying their body was ready for Rei-Punzel there is a liar. Damn.

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Concerns » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:13 am Jayfive wrote: Rei-Punzel

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:15 am A pun: Rei + Rapunzel = Reipunzel

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Archer » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:15 am Concerns wrote: What's that?

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Concerns » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:15 am Archer wrote: This is discussed a bit in, I wanna say the train station thread?

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Archer » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:20 am Konja7 wrote: However, they still mantain that Rei II and Rei Q are separate entities at the end (even if Rei Q's soul is Rei II). In the Instrumentality, Shinji still needs to convince Rei II that she could find her own place. Shinji even mentioned that Rei Q found her own place in Village-3 as a proof. I think the interpretation that Rei's seiyuu said in a interview is correct. Rei 2 and Rei Q are different people even if both are Rei Ayanami.

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Fri Aug 20, 2021 11:27 am I wonder if the similarities between Rei Q and Poka-Poka Rei are a way of showcasing how people are both unique and similar to each other at the same time-there's certainly other examples too (the similarities between Shinji and Asuka or Misato and Gendo in NTE were as intentional as Gendo's role as a grown-up Shinji who never matured in EoE.)

[]: Re: Why is Rei holding a doll at the end of NTE 3.0 + 1.0?             Quote Post by pir2confusion » Sat Aug 21, 2021 10:40 am With the name on the doll as opposed to just holding a baby I really think the only way to read that is the two are linked. I can't speak with any certainty but in the tv series the memories were copied from clone to clone. However Rei made a point of how she knew it wasn't her memories and the visual images show the clones as being different, looking at each other as unique entities. In Rebuild the memories in no way could have been copied by NERV and without living that experience with Tsubame this Rei still has it as part of her that she holds onto. So like the different evas were combined to form one, Rei also gets combined inside guf the place where all the souls are kept. The space is also made to look like the mocap studio that the movie was filmed in, so Rei could be like an actress and plays the part of the different versions of Rei. With all the different versions of the story or loops are just the same people as characters acting out the play in slightly different ways but now they have reached the end(of evas, of the movie, of the series) of the repeat or loop : | | and they each leave the stage.
